content: Comparative performance differences between ICM, RND, and traditional curious agents in curiosity-driven reinforcement learning can be systematically explained by how each agent’s native episodic memory architecture from Topic A interacts with its active learning curiosity threshold. We hypothesize that agents with decoupled long-term episodic memory modules (from neural architecture designs outlined in partial Topic A findings) explain why RND consistently outperforms ICM on sparse-reward long-horizon tasks by avoiding the 'curiosity collapse that plagues simpler curious agents with bounded memory. This is because memory structure, not the intrinsic reward formula itself, is the hidden moderating variable that mediates the observed performance gaps.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
